untranslatable_reason: This is a pairs trading/market-neutral strategy (long IGE + short SPY with equal dollar amounts) that requires multi-asset position management and correlation-based hedging logic. The backtest engine appears designed for single-instrument technical analysis. The strategy lacks quantifiable entry triggers based on price/indicator conditions—it is fundamentally a portfolio construction rule rather than a pattern-based trading signal. Exit date (November 2007) is a calendar constraint, not a technical condition. The source code snippet shows daily return calculations but no specific entry logic. Cannot express equal-dollar-amount hedging, multi-asset coordination, or the underlying economic thesis within the single-instrument indicator framework provided.
translation_notes: Signal requires: (1) two instruments traded simultaneously in opposite directions, (2) position sizing based on dollar amounts across assets, (3) correlation/hedge ratio management—none expressible in current schema. Entry conditions reference specific stocks (IGE/SPY) by name rather than technical patterns. Exit is time-based (November 2007) rather than price/indicator-based. Recommend: redesign backtest engine to support multi-leg strategies, or split into separate long/short signals with external position-sizing logic.
